m_Item1.QuestionText: In ARIMA(p,d,q) models, the parameter p stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: The p parameter represents the number of lagged observations included in the model. It refers to the number of autoregressive terms used to predict the current value of the series based on past values.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In ARIMA(p,d,q) models, the parameter d stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: The d parameter represents the number of times the series needs to be differenced to achieve stationarity.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In ARIMA(p,d,q) models, the parameter q stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: The q parameter represents the number of lagged forecast errors included in the model. It refers to the number of moving average terms used to predict the current value of the series based on the errors of the past forecasts.

m_Item1.QuestionText: For all the time series algorithms for measuring the accuracy of the predictions which of the evaluation metrics can be used?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Mean Square Error(MSE) is a metrics used to measure the accuracy of the predictions made by time series algorithms.

Options.OptionText: The forecast is the dot product of the decreasing weights(smoothing constant) and previous values
The forecast is the average of the previous values
The forecast is the average of the decreasing weights(smoothing constant)
The forecast is the average of previous some(span) values

m_Item1.Explanation: In simple exponential smoothing, each forecast is calculated as a weighted average of the previous observation and the previous forecast.

Options.OptionText: The forecast is the dot product of the decreasing weights(smoothing constant) and previous values
The forecast is the average of the previous values
The forecast is the average of the decreasing weights(smoothing constant)
The forecast is the average of previous some(span) values

m_Item1.Explanation: Moving average involves calculating the average of a fixed number of consecutive data points.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are _______ smoothing constants for Holt’s Linear Trend Method(without damping)
m_Item1.Explanation: In Holt's Linear Trend method, there are two smoothing constants that control the level and trend components of the forecast.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are _______ smoothing constants for Holt-Winter’s Method(without damping)
m_Item1.Explanation: The Holt-Winter's method is also known as triple exponential smoothing. In this method there are three smoothing constants used to forecast time series data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not true for forecasting?

Options.OptionText: The underlying casual system will remain same in the future
Forecast for group of items is accurate than individual item
Short range forecasts are less accurate than long range forecasts

m_Item1.Explanation: Short range forecasts are generally considered to be more accurate than long range forecasts.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a forecasting technique?
m_Item1.Explanation: Time horizon refers to the duration or period over which future predictions or estimates are made.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which of the following forecasting technique, subjective inputs obtained from various sources are analyzed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Judgemental forecasting refers to the process of making predictions based on the knowledge, expertise and judgement of individuals or a group of experts.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which of the following forecasting technique, data obtained from past experience is analyzed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Time series forecasting refers to the process of making predictions or estimates about future values based on patterns and trends observed in historical data collected over a specific time period.
The delphi method is a technique used to gatherand consolidate expert opinions or judgements on a particular topic.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Short term regular variations related to the calendar or time of day is known as
m_Item1.Explanation: Seasonality refers to the characteristics pattern or variation in a time series data that repeats over regular intervals.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In linear programming, oil companies used to implement resources available is classified as
m_Item1.Explanation: In linear programming, transportation model are applied to problems related to the study of efficient transportation routes.
